reasoning: César Cielo holds the 50m freestyle world record set in 2009 during the "supersuit" era. Recently, in May 2025, Kristian Gkolomeev swam faster at the Enhanced Games while using performance-enhancing drugs and a banned supersuit. According to FINA Rule SW12, records require a negative doping test and approved swimwear to be ratified. The Enhanced Games are unsanctioned and explicitly allow doping, meaning this time will not be ratified by World Aquatics and will not replace Cielo's record on Wikipedia's official list. Looking forward, the record has stood for 16 years and no clean swimmers have approached it recently. Even with upcoming competitions like the World Championships, supersuit records remain notoriously difficult to break. Therefore, it is highly probable that Cielo will retain the official record through all resolution dates, with slightly decreasing certainty over time to account for the minuscule chance of a legitimate breakthrough.
